ccel.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second
ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.text: Whence, again, has Sylvanus appeared to be a god?
Perhaps it is agreeable so to call him from this, that the pipe sings sweetly because he bestows the wood; for, perhaps, it might not be so.
Thou hast bought a venal master, when thou shalt have bought from him.
Art thou not ashamed, O fool, to adore such pictures?
Seek one God who will allow you to live after death.
Depart from such as have become dead in life.
